source: Command Doctor Mission Studio Level 0 visual registry

assets.title: Devices sharing a service
assets.alt_text: Laptop and tablet connected through a switch and gateway to a booking service.
assets.text_alternative: A network is shown as end devices connected to a switch, then a gateway, then a service. The important evidence is that devices exchange useful information through links.
evidence_requirements: Identify at least two connected devices.
Identify the shared service the devices are trying to reach.
Explain that the links carry requests and responses.

assets.title: Request and response path
assets.alt_text: Point of sale terminal sending a request to a payment service and receiving an approval response.
assets.text_alternative: The terminal asks for a service, the network carries the request, the server answers, and the terminal can continue. Communication has a purpose and a direction.
evidence_requirements: Explain why the response matters to the user.

assets.title: LAN, WAN, and internet scope
assets.alt_text: Local office LAN connected through a WAN or ISP path to an internet service.
assets.text_alternative: The LAN is the local room or site, the WAN links sites or providers, and the internet reaches public services. The same symptom can have different scope.
evidence_requirements: Separate a local printer or room issue from an internet issue.
Identify the boundary between local network and provider path.
Explain why scope changes the next evidence to collect.

assets.title: Simulation versus production boundary
assets.alt_text: Local simulated switch separated from a production network by a clear safety boundary.
assets.text_alternative: The local simulator is safe practice space. A production switch affects real guests, staff, and services, so the learner must keep that boundary clear.
evidence_requirements: Identify which side is local simulation.
Identify which side is production impact.
Explain why a first attempt belongs in simulation.
